reasoning: The reference value is very likely 3.67% (the Aug 1 Friday close prior to Aug 3). On Aug 1, the 3-year yield plummeted 22 basis points due to a disastrous July jobs report (missing expectations with catastrophic downward revisions for May and June), the firing of the BLS Commissioner, the resignation of a Fed governor, and newly announced tariffs. This unprecedented confluence of events drove massive safe-haven inflows and aggressively heightened expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts. While yields often mean-revert after extreme 1-day drops, the severity of the macroeconomic and political shock establishes a strong downward bias in the near term. P(>3.67) for Aug 10 is estimated at 0.42, factoring in the possibility of a partial bounce. Progressing toward Sept exactly ahead of expected Fed cuts, yields should remain depressed. For longer horizons (Nov 2025 and Jan 2026), uncertainty increases significantly regarding whether the rate cuts and tariffs will eventually spur inflation and a yield rebound, pushing probabilities steadily closer to 0.5.
